pros: Reduces average schedule creation time by 68% for multi-location retail chains (based on Deputy’s 2023 customer benchmark study of 142 clients averaging 37 employees per location)
Achieves 99.2% time clock accuracy via dual-verification (GPS geofencing + device-based biometrics), cutting time theft incidents by up to 41% in hospitality deployments
Automatically flags 94% of potential FLSA, state wage-hour, and local predictive scheduling violations before publishing schedules (validated against 2023 SHRM compliance audit data)
Integrates with 32+ payroll providers including ADP, Gusto, and Paychex, enabling error-free exports with <0.3% reconciliation variance across 98% of client pay cycles
Delivers real-time labor cost visibility down to the 15-minute shift segment, helping users reduce overstaffing by 12–18% on average (per Deputy ROI report Q2 2024)
Supports 17 languages and regional compliance templates for 14 countries, enabling global rollouts with <2 weeks of configuration for enterprises like Accor and JLL
Enables staff self-service shift swaps with automated manager approval workflows, reducing manual admin tasks by 7.2 hours/week per store manager (Deputy internal usage analytics, 2024)
cons: No native HRIS or benefits administration module — requires integration with BambooHR or Workday for full employee lifecycle management
Advanced forecasting features (e.g., AI-driven demand-based staffing) require minimum 50-employee subscription tier and are unavailable on Starter plans
Mobile app offline functionality is limited to viewing schedules; clock-in/clock-out and shift swaps require stable internet connection
Custom report builder has a 5-report limit on Professional tier and lacks SQL-level query access or API-driven export automation
features: Automated Compliance Alerts (FLSA, CA, NY, UK, AU)
Forecasting Engine with Historical Trend Analysis
KPI Tracking (Labor %, Overtime Rate, Schedule Adherence)
pricingDetail: Starts at $3.25/month per employee for the Starter plan (includes core scheduling, time tracking, and basic compliance); Professional tier ($5.95/employee) adds forecasting, advanced reporting, and priority support; Enterprise plans begin at $7.50/employee with SLA guarantees, dedicated CSM, and custom compliance rule sets.
useCase: Deputy is purpose-built for mid-market frontline organizations with 25–500+ employees operating across multiple locations—especially in retail, quick-service restaurants, healthcare clinics, and facilities management. These customers need granular labor compliance enforcement across jurisdictions, real-time visibility into labor costs against sales or patient volume, and scalable tools that empower hourly workers while reducing managerial overhead. They typically lack in-house IT resources but require enterprise-grade reliability, SOC 2 Type II certification, and seamless payroll integration without custom development.

userQuotes.quote: We manage 22 coastal resorts with seasonal staffing spikes—Deputy’s forecasting engine cut our last-minute hiring costs by 31% and kept us within 2.4% of budgeted labor spend across Q3 2023.
After implementing Deputy’s break compliance guardrails, we achieved 100% adherence across 47 clinics for 8 consecutive months—avoiding an estimated $89K in California wage-and-hour penalties.

I oversee 142 drivers across three depots. With Deputy’s role-based permissions and instant push alerts, I resolved 83% of schedule conflicts before first shift—and reduced no-call/no-shows by 27% in six months.
